TABLE II: Ablation and robustness analysis.
Fig. 2: The HSI cube is partitioned into 3D patches, embedded into a token matrix, projected into query, key, and value representations, normalized, and scored using a cosine-based overlap function.

Fig. 1: Standard dot-product attention mixes vector magnitude and angular alignment through q⊤k. While after ℓ2 normalization, the embeddings lie on a unit hypersphere and the score depends only on angular similarity. The proposed squared cosine score further sharpens the distinction between strongly aligned and weakly aligned token pairs.
